name: Parque Natural de Bertiz – circular from Oronoz-Mugairi via Aizkolegi path
name_original: Parque Natural de Bertiz – circular desde Oronoz-Mugairi por senda Aizkolegi
region_intro: Hiking around Bertizarana offers access to well-preserved natural landscapes within the Baztan-Bidasoa region of northern Navarre, Spain. The area is characterized by extensive Atlantic forests dominated by oak and beech trees, particularly within the Señorío de Bertiz Natural Park. Hikers can explore diverse ecosystems, including the summit of Mount Aizkolegi, which provides views of the park and surrounding valleys. The Bidasoa river also flows through the valley, adding to the varied terrain. Best hiking trails around Bertizarana The most popular hiking route is Parque Natural de Bertiz – circular from Oronoz-Mugairi via Aizkolegi path, a 5.5 miles (8.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 31 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the natural park with views towards Aizkolegi. Another top favourite among local hikers is Charcoal kilns – Bertiz Natural Park loop from Bertizarana, a moderate 7.6 miles (12.2 km) path. This trail leads through the natural park, highlighting historical charcoal kilns. Local hikers also love the Lime Kiln – Charcoal kilns loop from Bertizarana, a 14.3 miles (23.0 km) trail leading through dense Atlantic forests, often completed in about 6 hours 47 minutes. Hiking around Bertizarana is defined by extensive Atlantic forests, the summit of Mount Aizkolegi, and historical gardens. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely strolls to more challenging treks. The routes in Bertizarana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 3,900 hikers have used komoot to explore Bertizarana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Bertizarana?
A: Bertizarana offers a diverse network of over 15 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 4 easy, 11 moderate, and 1 difficult trail, ensuring options for everyone from casual walkers to experienced trekkers.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Bertizarana?
A: Hikers in Bertizarana will primarily explore the extensive and well-preserved Atlantic forests, dominated by impressive oak and beech trees, especially within the Señorío de Bertiz Natural Park. The region also features the Bidasoa river valley and offers magnificent views from higher points like Mount Aizkolegi.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for families or beginners in Bertizarana?
A: Yes, Bertizarana has 4 easy hiking trails. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31925925" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aizkolegi (841 m) – Aizkolegi Abandoned House loop from Baztan-Bidasoa&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.2 km long and takes around 1 hour 47 minutes to complete, offering a gentle introduction to the park's beauty.

Q: What historical or cultural sites can I visit along the trails in Bertizarana?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, Bertizarana offers several historical and cultural points of interest. Within the Señorío de Bertiz Natural Park, you can explore the ruins of a former summer palace atop Mount Aizkolegi and the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.visitnavarra.es/en/bertiz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historical-Artistic Garden&lt;/a&gt;. The villages of Oieregi, Narbarte, and Legasa also feature Romanesque bridges, traditional houses, and churches like San Juan Bautista and San Andrés.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Bertizarana?
A: Yes, many trails in Bertizarana are circular. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968771800/parque-natural-de-bertiz-circular-from-oronoz-mugairi-via-aizkolegi-path" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de Bertiz – circular from Oronoz-Mugairi via Aizkolegi path&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.8 km loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10518091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charcoal kilns – Bertiz Natural Park loop from Bertizarana&lt;/a&gt;, a 12.2 km moderate path that highlights historical charcoal kilns.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Bertizarana?
A: The temperate and rainy climate of Bertizarana, with minimal winter frosts, makes it suitable for hiking for much of the year. The Atlantic forests are particularly beautiful in spring with fresh greenery and in autumn with vibrant fall colors. Summer offers pleasant conditions for exploring, while winter can provide a unique, tranquil experience, though some trails might be muddy.

Q: Are there any trails that offer great viewpoints in Bertizarana?
A: Absolutely. The summit of Mount Aizkolegi, the highest point in the Señorío de Bertiz Natural Park, provides magnificent panoramic views of the park and surrounding valleys. Several trails lead towards or around this area, offering rewarding vistas.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Bertizarana?
A: The hiking routes in Bertizarana are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 500 reviews. Hikers often praise the well-preserved natural landscapes, the tranquility of the Atlantic forests, and the variety of trails available for different abilities.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Bertizarana?
A: While specific parking details for every trailhead are not always listed, the Señorío de Bertiz Natural Park, which is the main hiking area, typically provides designated parking facilities for visitors. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: Can I find any challenging hikes in Bertizarana?
A: Yes, Bertizarana offers challenging options for experienced hikers. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15007222" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lime Kiln – Charcoal kilns loop from Bertizarana&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 23 km trail with significant elevation gain, often taking nearly 7 hours to complete as it leads through dense Atlantic forests.

Q: Are there any nearby attractions or peaks worth exploring after a hike?
A: Beyond Bertizarana, you can explore several nearby attractions and peaks. Notable peaks include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5541077" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibanteli&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1098435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mendaur Summit and Hermitage&lt;/a&gt;. Other points of interest include the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3093165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elizondo Town Center&lt;/a&gt; and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3065940" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan Xar Cave Shrine&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: This beautiful and picturesque bridge is located over the bed of the Bidasoa river, one of the most important in Navarra. This bridge connects the town of Oronoz-Mugairi with the Bertiz Natural Park.

Declared a Natural Park in 1984, this beautiful forest in the Cantabrian Valleys is a place that offers unique tranquility. Walking through its various paths is a real pleasure, its dense grove of oaks and other species make each corner a true postcard.
